home.tagline: Sculpture, public art, watercolours and scenography since 1963
home.about_text: Sivert Lindblom (b. 1931) is one of Sweden's most significant sculptors. He studied at the Royal Academy of Fine Arts 1958–1963 and has since created an extensive body of sculptures, public installations, watercolours and scenography.
home.contact_sub: Questions about works, loans or collaborations

portfolio.title: Artistic work 1961–2016
portfolio.subtitle: Four decades of artistic work
portfolio.intro: Four categories document Sivert Lindblom's artistic practice: exhibitions, public commissions, watercolours and scenography.
portfolio.desc_exhibitions: Solo and group exhibitions in Sweden and internationally, from Galerie Buren 1963 to VANDALORUM 2016.
portfolio.desc_public: Sculptures and installations in public squares, metro stations, embassies and libraries in Sweden and around the world.
portfolio.desc_watercolors: Approx. 200 watercolour works — a 'mental constructivism' project featuring hermetic architectural geometry.
portfolio.desc_scenography: Set designs for Stockholms Stadsteater and choreographies with Margareta Åsberg and others.

watercolors.title: Watercolours 1975–2012
watercolors.description: Approximately 200 sketch-like watercolour works created as a form of 'mental constructivism', depicting a hermetic architectural landscape. The works are shaped by a strictly controlled graphic technique resulting in a freely applied axonometry.
watercolors.book_note: Fifty works were exhibited at the Royal Academy of Fine Arts in Stockholm, 6 October – 4 November 2012. The book Sivert Lindblom Watercolours etc. (Bullfinch Publishing, 2012) documents the collection.

texts.title: Criticism, essays & interviews
texts.subtitle: Texts
texts.intro: Texts about and by Sivert Lindblom: critical essays, reviews, interviews and his own writings, 1963–2012.

biography.title: Sivert Lindblom
biography.intro: Sivert Lindblom (b. 1931 in Husby-Rekarne, Södermanland) is one of Sweden's most significant sculptors. He studied at the Royal Academy of Fine Arts in Stockholm 1958–1963 and has since created an extensive body of sculptures, public installations, watercolours and scenography.
